- Problem focus (データ選択の効率性): Modern subset selection breaks at web scale due to repeated retraining/re-scoring (SELCON/GLISTER), multi-seed averaging (EL2N/GraNd), or second-order solvers (influence/pruning). Operations teams need a single warm-up, single validation alignment, and a single, bounded-memory pass that yields nested subsets for any budget, compatible with continual refresh and privacy.
- Gap vs prior work:
  • Dynamics: Early-loss snapshots (EL2N/GraNd) ignore temporal geometry of SGD; influence/pruning uses costly H−1 approximations detached from streaming needs.
  • Objectives: Most methods optimize training proxies, not multi-objective validation targets (accuracy, fairness, calibration) in one pass.
  • Reusability: Few methods return budget-robust, nested subsets; AutoCoreset iterates over loss matrices; SELCON needs repeated Train calls.
- Why now: Foundation-model finetuning (LoRA/linear-head), streaming data, and hardware favor linear-time sketching and matrix–vector ops over iterative selection. A once-for-all, streaming, validation-aligned selector unlocks practical deployment at web scale with DP and continual updates.

Methodology
We propose One-Pass Sketch-and-Validate (OP-S&V): a streaming, validation-aligned, nested coreset builder based on temporal gradient sketches and an online dual controller. It is designed to be plug-in for PyTorch/JAX pipelines and FM finetuning.

Core pipeline (single warm-up, single validation pass, single streaming pass)
1) Temporal gradient sketching (short warm-up; bounded memory)
- Per-example head (or LoRA) gradients g_{i,t} from a brief warm-up. Optional deeper coverage via s randomized vJPs; compress each to d̂ with CountSketch/OSNAP and 8–16 bit quantization.
- Robust Frequent Directions (RFD) with exponential forgetting tracks the time-varying gradient subspace U ∈ R^{d̂×r}. Dynamic-rank scheduling increases r until spectral tail < ε_spec. Compute/memory: O(nd̂) time, O(r·d̂) memory; runs fused on GPU.
- New vs draft: Temporal drift index. Maintain Δ_spec = ||U_t U_t^T − U_{t−1} U_{t−1}^T||_F as a cheap drift detector that gates when to refresh validation signals under distribution shift.

2) Single-shot validation-aligned control (one backprop per objective)
- Compute validation gradients v_q = ∇_θ L_val,q(θ_T) for q in {accuracy, per-group fairness, differentiable calibration (temperature/ECE surrogate)}.
- Project and stabilize: v̂_q = Proj_head ∘ Proj_U(P v_q) where P is light preconditioner (diag Fisher or block K-FAC built from warm-up batches); no H−1 solves.
- New vs prior: Multi-objective, constraint-first alignment. Specify target descent budgets τ_q and align selection probabilities so expected gradient contribution achieves Σ_i p_i⟨g_{i,T}, P v̂_q⟩ ≤ −τ_q for all q, avoiding bilevel loops (SELCON/GLISTER).

3) One-pass scoring with online dual control
- Temporal novelty: r_i = Σ_t ||(I − U U^T) g_{i,t}||² (late-t approximation allowed).
- Validation impact per objective: a_{i,q} = max(−⟨g_{i,T}, P v̂_q⟩, 0)/(||P v̂_q||+ε).
- Combined score: s_i(λ) = r_i + Σ_q λ_q a_{i,q} with a small sampling floor γ/n.
- Streaming λ updates: Maintain running estimates of E_p[⟨g, P v̂_q⟩] while scanning once; update λ_q via projected subgradient with closed-form step sizes from Lipschitz bounds on a_{i,q}. Guarantees feasibility in one pass without retraining.
- New vs draft: Stability guard. If variance of running estimate exceeds a threshold, temporarily widen γ to avoid over-concentrated sampling and resume once estimates stabilize.

4) Nested sampling and budget-free auto-stop
- Fixed-key weighted-reservoir sampling on s_i yields nestedness: larger budgets are supersets. Weights w_i = 1/(m p_i) with percentile clipping for optimizer stability.
- Auto-stop criteria: stop when (i) RFD spectral tail < ε_spec and (ii) projected validation residuals ||Π_U P v̂_q|| < ε_val for all q. This emits a once-for-all nested coreset without pre-specifying m.
- New vs prior: Mergeable coreset-of-coresets. Priority-union operator merges reservoirs from different shards/streams while preserving nestedness and unbiasedness.

5) Continual, fairness-aware, and DP-compliant
- Continual refresh: Keep RFD rolling with forgetting; recompute v̂_q on schedule or drift trigger Δ_spec > κ. Only score new data; union reservoirs.
- Fairness/coverage: Add per-group probability floors or hinge penalties in s_i; include group-specific validation gradients v_q to prevent collapse.
- Differential privacy: Add Gaussian noise to sketch updates and to v_q before projection; release only U and {(i, w_i)}. Privacy accounting tight due to low-rank bounded releases.

6) Theory and diagnostics
- Sketch preservation: RFD guarantees preserve gradient energy up to ε_spec; first-order updates within span(U) stable.
- Validation descent: If weighted moment matching in projected space holds within ξ, each objective decreases by at least η⟨Σ_i w_i g_{i,T}, P v̂_q⟩ − O(ξ²). Priority sampling concentration bounds control variance under floors.
- New: Drift-triggered regret. Bound cumulative validation regret between refreshes in terms of Δ_spec and refresh interval, yielding an auto-refresh policy with sublinear regret.

7) Practical additions and code changes (PyTorch/JAX)
- New modules:
  • GradientSketcher: fused CountSketch + quantization + RFD with forgetting; exposes U and Δ_spec.
  • ValAligner: computes v_q via a single autograd per objective; applies P and projections into U and head.
  • DualController: streaming λ updates with running moment trackers and stability guard; exposes p_i on-the-fly.
  • NestedReservoir: fixed-key weighted reservoir with priority-union and DP wrappers.
  • Diagnostics: spectral tail, moment residuals, drift index, λ trajectories, group coverage.
- Minimal hooks: register per-example head/LoRA gradients; optional s vJPs; one validation backward per objective.
- FM-friendly mode: restrict gradients to linear head or LoRA adapters; U then lives in a tiny parameter space while still steering selection for later full/partial finetuning.

How OP-S&V differs from prior studies
- vs SELCON: no iterative Train calls or MM loops; no submodular surrogates; streaming, single-pass λ control aligns directly to multi-objective validation without bilevel optimization; returns nested, once-for-all coresets and auto-stop.
- vs EL2N/GraNd: uses temporal gradient geometry instead of early loss-only proxies; eliminates multi-seed averaging; incorporates validation targets (accuracy, fairness, calibration) and preconditioning.
- vs AutoCoreset: avoids building an n×t loss matrix; uses linear-time gradient sketches and priority sampling; guarantees nestedness and budget-free auto-stop.
- vs Influence/pruning: replaces H−1 solves with light preconditioning and low-rank projected alignment; no simulated annealing or expensive second order.
- vs Monotonic-kernel coresets: not restricted to monotone regularized losses; supports deep nets and non-monotone, multi-objective validation criteria.
- vs Dataset distillation: constructs real-data, weighted nested subsets; no meta-optimization or synthetic data.
- vs Prune4ReL and active learning: targets efficient training subset selection (not relabeling or label acquisition) and operates in one streaming pass with multi-objective validation control.

Expected impact
- Efficiency and simplicity: Order-of-magnitude reduction in selection time/memory via single warm-up + single validation pass + single streaming pass; bounded memory via sketches; one artifact reusable across any budget due to nestedness.
- Quality at small budgets: Temporal geometry preserves optimization-relevant directions; validation alignment steers accuracy, fairness, and calibration simultaneously; robust under continual refresh with drift triggers.
- Operational robustness: Budget-free auto-stop; diagnostics are actionable (spectral tail, residuals, drift); mergeable reservoirs support sharded/streaming data engineering workflows.
- Broad applicability: Deep/shallow classification and regression; foundation-model finetuning (head/LoRA); privacy-sensitive pipelines via DP OP-S&V; fairness-aware selection with explicit group controls.
- Deployment readiness: Minimal code intrusions (a few hooks/modules), hardware-friendly fused ops, and compatibility with existing PyTorch/JAX stacks enable adoption in production and large-scale research settings.

experimental_design.experiment_strategy:
Experiment 1 — Accuracy/Calibration/Efficiency on Images (nested, single-pass)
Goal
- Demonstrate that OP-S&V produces nested subsets that achieve higher accuracy and better calibration than baselines at the same budget, while being faster and more memory-efficient.

Setup
- Dataset: CIFAR-100 (50k train, 10k test). Optionally Tiny-ImageNet for a larger variant.
- Model: Frozen CLIP ViT-B/32 encoder + trainable linear head (512 -> 100). This keeps per-sample gradients trivial and GPU-friendly.
- Hardware: 1x A100 or 3090 (works on a single GPU). Batch size 512.

OP-S&V configuration
- Warm-up: 1 epoch on a 10% random slice. Capture per-sample head gradients at a few checkpoints (e.g., 3 mini-epochs). Quantize to 8-bit.
- Sketch: Random CountSketch to d_hat=1024 followed by Robust Frequent Directions (RFD) with forgetting factor 0.98. Dynamic rank r up to 64 until spectral tail < eps_spec=0.05.
- Validation: 5k held-out from train. Objectives: accuracy (cross-entropy) and calibration (ECE surrogate via differentiable temperature scaling). Compute v_acc, v_cal by a single backward each.
- Preconditioner P: diag Fisher on the head from warm-up batches; fallback to identity.
- Controller: λ = [λ_acc, λ_cal] initialized to 1.0; project to [0,10]. Stability guard widens γ (sampling floor) from 5e-4 to 5e-3 when variance spikes.
- Streaming pass: one linear-time pass over remaining training data to score s_i and feed NestedReservoir with fixed keys; budgets evaluated at {1%, 5%, 10%, 25%, 50%} (nested by construction). Auto-stop enabled using eps_spec and eps_val=0.02.

Baselines (Python-feasible)
- Random subset per budget (nested via fixed seeds but not guaranteed strict nestedness).
- LossTop-k: pick top-k samples by latest cross-entropy from warm-up.
- EntropyTop-k: top-k predictive entropy.
- EL2N: mean loss over first 1 epoch (no multi-seed); GraNd: per-sample grad-norm at early epoch. Both computed once.
- Herding (k-center) on CLIP embeddings using Faiss or sklearn k-center greedy.

Metrics
- Test accuracy and NLL on CIFAR-100 test set after finetuning only the head for 30 epochs on the selected subset (same optimizer, cosine LR, weight decay 0.01).
- ECE (15-bin) with temperature optimized on validation.
- Selection time (wall-clock), peak GPU memory during selection.
- Nestedness: check that subset@B is a strict superset of subset@b for B>b (100% for OP-S&V, report violations for baselines).
- Ablations: remove temporal novelty r_i; use identity vs Fisher P; single-objective vs multi-objective.
- Statistics: 3 seeds, report mean±95% CI. Paired t-test vs strongest baseline per budget.

Procedure (high level)
1) Warm-up head for 1 epoch on 10% slice; cache per-sample head grad snapshots, project via CountSketch, feed RFD with forgetting, compute Δ_spec for drift diag.
2) Compute validation gradients v_acc, v_cal; precondition and project to span(U) and head.
3) Single streaming pass over train: compute late-time head gradients g_{i,T}, temporal novelty r_i, validation impacts a_{i,q}, update λ online, push (i, weight) to nested reservoir using fixed keys.
4) For each budget, finetune head on weighted subset (same epochs), evaluate metrics.
5) Compare to baselines with identical finetuning recipe.

Key expected outcomes
- OP-S&V dominates accuracy-NLL-ECE trade-off at small budgets (1–10%), maintaining or improving ECE vs baselines.
- Selection time within 1.2–1.5x of Random and 3–10x faster than Herding; memory bounded by O(r·d_hat) with r<=64.
- Ablation shows temporal novelty r_i boosts small-budget accuracy; Fisher P reduces NLL/ECE.

Minimal code sketch (PyTorch; linear head only) — selection

import torch, math, random
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader

# --- Utilities ---
def per_sample_head_grad(logits, y):
    # logits: [B, C], linear head parameters W: [C, D], per-sample grad wrt W is outer(residual, features)
    # But if we cache features f: [B, D] and logits = f @ W.T
    # Return flattened per-sample grads g_i (vectorized across classes)
    B, C = logits.shape
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=1)
    y_onehot = torch.nn.functional.one_hot(y, C).float()
    residual = probs - y_onehot  # [B, C]
    return residual  # later combine with features via kron

def countsketch(M, s_idx, s_sign):
    # M: [B, D], s_idx: [D] target buckets, s_sign: [D]
    B, D = M.shape
    d_hat = int(s_idx.max().item()) + 1
    out = M.new_zeros(B, d_hat)
    out.index_add_(1, s_idx, M * s_sign)
    return out

class RFD:
    def __init__(self, d_hat, r_max=64, forgetting=0.98):
        self.B = torch.zeros(d_hat, r_max)
        self.forgetting = forgetting
        self.eps_spec = 0.05
        self.rank = 0
        self.prev_proj = None
    @torch.no_grad()
    def update(self, X):
        # X: [B, d_hat]; apply forgetting and FD update (simplified FD)
        self.B *= math.sqrt(self.forgetting)
        # Append rows, do QR then shrink via SVD on small sketch
        Y = torch.cat([self.B, X.T], dim=1)  # [d_hat, r_max + B]
        Q, R = torch.linalg.qr(Y, mode='reduced')
        U, S, Vh = torch.linalg.svd(R, full_matrices=False)
        # Shrinkage
        tau = (S[self.rank-1]**2 if self.rank>0 else 0.0)
        S_shrink = torch.clamp(S**2 - tau, min=0.0).sqrt()
        keep = (S_shrink > 1e-6)
        k = min(keep.sum().item(), self.B.shape[1])
        self.rank = k
        self.B[:, :k] = (Q @ U[:, :k]) * S_shrink[:k]
        # Δ_spec approximation
        proj = (self.B[:, :k] @ torch.linalg.pinv(self.B[:,:k])) if k>0 else torch.zeros_like(self.B)
        if self.prev_proj is None:
            self.prev_proj = proj
        delta_spec = torch.linalg.norm(proj - self.prev_proj, ord='fro').item()
        self.prev_proj = proj
        tail = (S_shrink[k:]**2).sum().item() if k < len(S_shrink) else 0.0
        return tail, delta_spec
    def basis(self):
        k = max(self.rank, 1)
        Q, _ = torch.linalg.qr(self.B[:, :k], mode='reduced')
        return Q  # [d_hat, k]

class NestedReservoir:
    def __init__(self, max_k=50000, seed=0):
        self.max_k = max_k
        self.rng = random.Random(seed)
        self.heap = []  # store (-priority, idx, weight)
        self.fixed_u = {}
    def push(self, idx, weight, priority):
        import heapq
        item = (-priority, idx, weight)
        if len(self.heap) < self.max_k:
            heapq.heappush(self.heap, item)
        else:
            if item > self.heap[0]:
                heapq.heapreplace(self.heap, item)
    def items(self):
        return [(idx, w) for _, idx, w in sorted(self.heap)]

# --- Selection loop skeleton ---

def op_snv_select(dataloader_stream, features_fn, head, val_v_acc, val_v_cal, fisher_diag,
                  s_idx, s_sign, rfd: RFD, budgets=[0.01,0.05,0.1,0.25,0.5], gamma=5e-4):
    # Precompute projections of validation gradients
    # Flatten head params to vector; P ~ diag fisher
    P = 1.0 / (fisher_diag + 1e-6)
    def proj_vec(v):
        # CountSketch + RFD projection
        v_cs = countsketch(v[None, :], s_idx, s_sign)[0]
        U = rfd.basis()  # [d_hat, r]
        return (U @ (U.T @ v_cs))  # projected into span(U)

    v_acc_proj = proj_vec(val_v_acc)
    v_cal_proj = proj_vec(val_v_cal)
    norm_acc = v_acc_proj.norm() + 1e-8
    norm_cal = v_cal_proj.norm() + 1e-8

    lam_acc, lam_cal = 1.0, 1.0
    # running moments for controller
    m_acc, m_cal = 0.0, 0.0
    n_seen = 0

    max_k = int(sum(len(b) for b in dataloader_stream.dataset) * max(budgets)) if hasattr(dataloader_stream, 'dataset') else 25000
    reservoir = NestedReservoir(max_k=max_k)

    for batch in dataloader_stream:
        x, y, idx = batch[0].cuda(), batch[1].cuda(), batch[2]
        with torch.no_grad():
            f = features_fn(x)                 # [B, D]
            logits = f @ head.weight.t()       # [B, C]
            res = per_sample_head_grad(logits, y)  # [B, C]
            # flatten per-sample grad wrt head params: kron(res_i, f_i)
            g = torch.einsum('bc,bd->bcd', res, f).reshape(x.size(0), -1)  # [B, C*D]
            # CountSketch compress
            g_cs = countsketch(g, s_idx, s_sign)  # [B, d_hat]
            # novelty term r_i via residual outside span(U)
            U = rfd.basis()
            proj = g_cs @ U @ U.T
            novelty = (g_cs - proj).pow(2).sum(dim=1)  # [B]
            # validation impacts
            acc_imp = torch.clamp(-(g_cs @ v_acc_proj) / norm_acc, min=0)
            cal_imp = torch.clamp(-(g_cs @ v_cal_proj) / norm_cal, min=0)
            score = novelty + lam_acc * acc_imp + lam_cal * cal_imp
            # sampling probability floor
            p_floor = gamma
            # Fixed-key priority sampling: use a shared key seeded by idx for nestedness
            for i in range(x.size(0)):
                wi = 1.0  # initial weight, could be 1/(m p_i)
                key = (hash(int(idx[i])) & 0xffffffff) / 2**32
                priority = (score[i].item() + 1e-8) / max(p_floor, 1e-8) / max(key, 1e-9)
                reservoir.push(int(idx[i]), wi, priority)
            # controller update (projected subgradient, simplified)
            batch_acc = (g_cs @ v_acc_proj).mean().item()
            batch_cal = (g_cs @ v_cal_proj).mean().item()
            n_seen += x.size(0)
            m_acc = 0.9*m_acc + 0.1*batch_acc
            m_cal = 0.9*m_cal + 0.1*batch_cal
            # want E[<g, Pv_q>] <= -tau_q; subgradient step on λ
            tau_acc, tau_cal = -0.01, -0.005
            step = 0.1
            lam_acc = max(0.0, min(10.0, lam_acc + step * (m_acc - tau_acc)))
            lam_cal = max(0.0, min(10.0, lam_cal + step * (m_cal - tau_cal)))
    return reservoir

# Training/eval: for each budget, take top-K from reservoir and finetune head with sample weights


Experiment 2 — Multi-objective Fairness + Calibration on Text (constraint-first alignment)
Goal
- Show OP-S&V can target fairness and calibration simultaneously using a single validation pass and controller, outperforming accuracy-only selection at matched compute and budget.

Setup
- Dataset: CivilComments-WILDS (toxicity classification) with identity subgroups (e.g., gender, religion). Use the official train/val/test splits.
- Model: Frozen roberta-base encoder + trainable linear head.
- Objectives: accuracy (CE), group fairness surrogate (equalized odds via differentiable soft-TPR gap per group), calibration (ECE surrogate with temperature).

OP-S&V configuration
- Warm-up: 0.5 epoch on 20% of training.
- Sketch space: head gradients only; d_hat=2048 via CountSketch; RFD rank r<=64.
- Validation gradients: v_acc, {v_group for each protected group g}, v_cal.
- Per-group probability floors: ensure min 1% selection probability within each group.
- Controller λ: vector over [acc, cal, groups], updated via projected subgradient to meet target descent budgets τ_q; stability guard on.

Baselines
- Random (with stratification by group).
- LossTop-k (global) and GroupLossTop-k (per-group quotas).
- EL2N and GraNd computed once; per-group rebalancing by quotas.

Metrics
- Overall AUC/accuracy; subgroup AUC; worst-group accuracy; equalized odds gap (|TPR_g-TPR| averaged); ECE; selection time/memory.
- Pareto front between accuracy and worst-group accuracy at budgets {2%, 5%, 10%, 25%}.
- Ablations: with/without group-specific gradients; with/without probability floors.

Procedure
1) Warm-up and sketch U with forgetting.
2) Compute v_acc, v_cal, and v_group for each subgroup using differentiable soft-TPR: TPR ≈ mean(sigmoid(α·logit) on positives) with α=5; loss = sum_g |TPR_g - TPR_all|.
3) Single streaming pass with group-aware scoring and floors; build nested reservoir.
4) Finetune head on weighted subset; evaluate overall and worst-group metrics on test.

Key expected outcomes
- OP-S&V improves worst-group accuracy/EOD gap at small budgets without large accuracy loss vs accuracy-only baselines; better ECE.
- Floors prevent group collapse; group v_q further improves fairness versus quotas alone.

Minimal code sketch — computing group fairness gradient

# y in {0,1}, logits [N], group ids g in {0..G-1}
def soft_tpr(logits, y, mask, alpha=5.0):
    z = logits[mask]
    y_pos = (y[mask] == 1)
    if y_pos.sum() == 0:
        return logits.new_tensor(0.5)
    s = torch.sigmoid(alpha * z)
    return s[y_pos].mean()

def fairness_loss(logits, y, groups, G):
    masks = [groups==g for g in range(G)]
    tpr_all = soft_tpr(logits, y, torch.ones_like(y, dtype=torch.bool))
    loss = 0.0
    for g in range(G):
        tpr_g = soft_tpr(logits, y, masks[g])
        loss = loss + (tpr_g - tpr_all).abs()
    return loss / G

# Backprop once on validation to get v_group and v_acc, v_cal


Experiment 3 — Streaming, Drift, DP, and Mergeable Reservoirs (operational robustness)
Goal
- Validate continual refresh with drift-triggered validation re-alignment, mergeable reservoirs across shards, and DP-compliant selection with bounded utility loss.

Setup
- Dataset stream: CIFAR-10 then CIFAR-10C (severity 3 corruptions) to simulate distribution shift. Stream A: 40k clean; Stream B: 10k per corruption type (Gaussian noise, blur), arriving in shards of 5k.
- Model: Frozen CLIP ViT-B/32 encoder + head.
- Privacy: Gaussian noise to sketch updates and validation gradients; target (ε, δ) ~ (2.0, 1e-6) using moments accountant over T updates.

OP-S&V configuration
- RFD with forgetting 0.97; monitor Δ_spec. Trigger validation re-computation when Δ_spec > κ=0.2 or every 5k samples, whichever first.
- Controller resumes from last λ; widen γ during high-variance phases.
- NestedReservoir per shard with fixed-key; merge via priority-union into a global reservoir preserving nestedness.

Baselines
- Streaming EL2N recomputed every shard (no nestedness guarantee).
- Random streaming with periodic re-weighting.

Metrics
- Test accuracy on clean and corrupted test sets after finetuning on current reservoir at milestones (after each shard).
- Cumulative validation regret: sum_t L_val(θ_t) - L_val(θ*_t) approximated via projected validation residual norm.
- Δ_spec traces vs performance; number of refreshes; selection time/memory.
- DP utility: compare DP vs non-DP OP-S&V at budgets {5%, 10%, 25%}.

Procedure
1) Process Stream A shards to build reservoir_A. Finetune and evaluate after final shard.
2) As Stream B shards arrive, continue RFD with forgetting, compute Δ_spec; when triggered, recompute v_q on a small clean+corrupt validation mix and resume streaming controller.
3) For each shard, build local reservoir_s, merge with global via priority-union; evaluate at checkpoints.
4) Repeat with DP noise added to sketch updates and v_q; report utility gap.

Key expected outcomes
- Δ_spec spikes at shift; refresh reduces projected validation residual and recovers accuracy on corrupted test set.
- Mergeable reservoirs match single-stream selection within CI.
- DP variant shows small accuracy drop (<2–3 pp at 10% budget) while preserving nestedness.

Minimal code sketch — drift, DP, and merge

class DPSketchWrapper:
    def __init__(self, sigma):
        self.sigma = sigma
    def add_noise(self, X):
        return X + torch.randn_like(X) * self.sigma

def priority_union(resA: NestedReservoir, resB: NestedReservoir, K):
    import heapq
    merged = NestedReservoir(max_k=K)
    heap = resA.heap + resB.heap
    heapq.heapify(heap)
    while len(merged.heap) < K and heap:
        merged.heap.append(heapq.heappop(heap))
    return merged

# Drift-triggered refresh loop

def process_shard(shard_loader, rfd, dps, controller_state, reservoir_global, val_data):
    tail_prev = None
    for x,y,idx in shard_loader:
        # ... compute g_cs = countsketch(g, ...) then optionally add DP noise
        g_cs = countsketch(...)
        g_cs = dps.add_noise(g_cs)
        tail, delta = rfd.update(g_cs)
        if delta > 0.2:
            # recompute validation gradients on mixed val_data and reset controller moments
            controller_state.reset_moments()
        # compute scores, update lambda, push to local reservoir
    # after shard: priority-union local into global


Common implementation details and hyperparameters
- Optimizer for head finetuning: AdamW lr=2e-4, 30 epochs, cosine decay, wd=0.01, label smoothing 0.1.
- Temperature calibration: fit on validation by optimizing NLL over T in [0.5, 5.0] (grid or LBFGS).
- CountSketch: choose s_idx by hashing param index to [0, d_hat), s_sign by Rademacher; quantize g_cs to int8 with per-column scale.
- Fisher diag: average of squared per-sample grads across warm-up batches.
- Controller step sizes: use fixed step 0.1; clamp λ to [0,10]; τ targets tuned on val to change sign of projected residuals.

Reporting and sanity checks
- Verify nestedness explicitly.
- Track λ trajectories and moment residuals; widen γ when variance>threshold.
- Plot budget vs accuracy/NLL/ECE (Exp1), Pareto curves (Exp2), time/memory bar charts, Δ_spec and regret curves (Exp3).

Risks and mitigations
- If RFD numerics are unstable, fall back to a simpler Frequent Directions or incremental PCA.
- If per-sample grads are slow, restrict entirely to head grads (as specified) or use functorch.vmap; confirm throughput with B>=256.
- If fairness gradients are too noisy, increase validation size or smooth with EMA.

Reproducibility
- 3 seeds: 13, 17, 23. Fixed hashing for CountSketch and reservoir keys. Log spectral tail, Δ_spec, λ, and controller variance per 1k samples.
experimental_design.experiment_details:
Overview
I detail three experiments aligned with the Experiment Strategy and fully implementing the OP-S&V method in PyTorch. I emphasize reliability (multiple seeds, paired tests, logging, nestedness checks), use existing libraries where possible (open_clip, transformers, wilds, faiss/sklearn, opacus, scipy), and avoid redundant experiments by sharing components where appropriate (frozen encoders, linear heads, feature precomputation, unified training/eval utilities). Minimal code sketches are provided for key components; full training loops are kept concise to focus on OP-S&V.

Common implementation, reliability, and reproducibility settings (applies to all experiments unless noted)
- Frameworks and versions (example baseline stack):
  • torch>=2.2 (CUDA enabled), torchvision>=0.17
  • open_clip_torch>=2.24 or clip (for CLIP); transformers>=4.42 (for RoBERTa)
  • faiss-gpu>=1.7.4 (optional) or scikit-learn>=1.4 (for k-center/herding)
  • wilds>=2.0.0 (CivilComments-WILDS)
  • opacus>=1.4.0 (DP accountant/noise calibration)
  • scipy>=1.11, numpy>=1.26, scikit-image (CIFAR-10C generation, or use official CIFAR-10C)
- Determinism and seeds: use torch.manual_seed, numpy.random.seed, random.seed; set deterministic cudnn and torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(True) where throughput allows. Seeds: {13, 17, 23}.
- DataLoader: pin_memory=True, persistent_workers=True, num_workers tuned (e.g., 8). Shuffle only where specified.
- Feature precomputation: to maximize throughput and consistency, precompute frozen-encoder features for all train/val/test (and shards) to .pt files. Per-experiment modules operate on feature tensors rather than images/text directly. This also avoids augmentation confounds in subset selection.
- Training recipe (linear head): AdamW(lr=2e-4, wd=1e-2), cosine decay, label smoothing=0.1, batch size 512, 30 epochs (unless specified). Enable amp/autocast for throughput; scaler in training.
- Calibration: temperature T fitted post-training on validation via LBFGS or grid over [0.5, 5.0]; ECE computed with 15 bins using netcal or custom function.
- Statistical reporting: 3 seeds; report mean±95% CI; paired t-test (scipy.stats.ttest_rel) vs strongest baseline per budget.
- Implementation choices for OP-S&V components:
  • CountSketch: implemented using torch.index_add_ (fast, no external dep). Use fixed hash functions per seed: s_idx in [0, d_hat), s_sign in {±1} for each parameter index.
  • Robust Frequent Directions (RFD): use a lightweight torch implementation with small-r SVD on each update. As a practical fallback, scikit-learn IncrementalPCA with forgetting can approximate RFD if numerics are unstable; we log the switch when it happens.
  • Preconditioner P: diagonal Fisher estimated from warm-up batches (sum of squared per-sample head grads / N); fallback to identity.
  • Dual controller: projected subgradient with per-objective running moment estimates; λ clamped to [0,10]; sampling floor γ widened when moment variance>threshold.
  • Nested reservoir: fixed-key priority sampling (priority = score/key); union via priority-merge of heaps preserves nestedness.
- Logging: save spectral tail, Δ_spec, λ trajectories, moment residuals, p_floor changes, auto-stop decisions, GPU peak memory, selection and training wall-clock. Persist selection artifacts: U basis, s_idx/s_sign, Fisher diag, λ logs, reservoir heap (indices and weights) per seed.

Experiment 1 — Accuracy/Calibration/Efficiency on Images (nested, single-pass)
Goal
- Show that OP-S&V builds nested subsets that improve test accuracy and calibration (ECE, NLL) at fixed budgets while reducing selection time/memory versus baselines.

Setup
- Dataset: CIFAR-100 (50k train, 10k test). Validation: random 5k from train (disjoint from selection stream). Optionally replicate on Tiny-ImageNet.
- Model: frozen CLIP ViT-B/32 encoder via open_clip; trainable linear head (512→100). Features are 512-d pooled CLIP embeddings per image.
- Hardware: 1×A100 or 3090; batch size 512.

OP-S&V configuration (image)
- Warm-up: 1 epoch on a 10% random slice of train (5k samples); cache per-sample head gradients at 3 checkpoints (at 0.33, 0.66, 1.0 of warm-up). Quantize CountSketch outputs to int8 with per-column scales.
- Sketch: CountSketch compresses head-grad vectors into d_hat=1024; RFD with forgetting=0.98; dynamic rank r≤64 until spectral tail<eps_spec=0.05; compute Δ_spec each update to check drift (mostly stationary here).
- Validation objectives: accuracy (cross-entropy) and calibration (temperature-scaled NLL surrogate). Compute v_acc and v_cal by one backward pass each on the 5k validation set using current head; project with P and into U.
- Preconditioner P: diagonal Fisher on head from warm-up; identity if poorly conditioned.
- Controller: λ=[λ_acc, λ_cal] init=1.0; τ targets tuned on small held-out split to encourage negative projected residuals (e.g., τ_acc=0.01, τ_cal=0.005). λ∈[0,10]. Stability guard: widen γ from 5e-4 to 5e-3 when running variance of projected moments exceeds 2× median of last 5 windows.
- Streaming pass: one linear pass over the remaining 45k training samples. Compute late-time head gradients g_{i,T}, novelty r_i, impacts a_{i,acc}, a_{i,cal}; update λ online; push to NestedReservoir with fixed keys. Budgets evaluated post hoc at {1%, 5%, 10%, 25%, 50%}; nested by construction. Auto-stop enabled with eps_spec=0.05 and eps_val=0.02.

Baselines
- Random subsets per budget (three seeds, same seeds as OP-S&V).
- LossTop-k: top-k by cross-entropy computed after warm-up.
- EntropyTop-k: top-k predictive entropy after warm-up.
- EL2N and GraNd computed once after first warm-up epoch (no multi-seed averaging):
  • EL2N: mean CE over the warm-up epoch.
  • GraNd: per-sample head grad norm at warm-up end.
- Herding (k-center greedy) on CLIP features using scikit-learn or faiss; distance metric cosine; greedy selection to k elements per budget.

Metrics
- Accuracy and NLL on CIFAR-100 test after training only the head for 30 epochs on each selected subset; use sample weights from reservoir (weights w_i = 1/(m p_i) with percentile clipping at 95th for optimizer stability).
- ECE (15 bins) after post-hoc temperature scaling on validation.
- Selection time and peak GPU memory.
- Nestedness: fraction of violations that subset@B is not a superset of subset@b across budgets (expect 0 for OP-S&V).
- Ablations: remove r_i; replace P with identity; single-objective alignment (accuracy-only) versus multi-objective.
- Stats: 3 seeds; paired t-test vs strongest baseline per budget.

Procedure
1) Feature extraction: use open_clip ViT-B/32; precompute 512-d features for train/val/test; store to disk.
2) Warm-up on 10% slice: train head for 1 epoch; at 3 equally spaced checkpoints, compute per-sample head grads on the warm-up slice and feed the CountSketch→RFD pipeline with forgetting; estimate diagonal Fisher. Quantize sketches.
3) Validation gradients: run two independent backward passes on validation to get v_acc and v_cal; apply P and project into span(U) and head.
4) Single streaming pass: on the remaining 45k samples, compute per-sample head grads at late time g_{i,T}, novelty r_i via (I−UU^T), impacts a_{i,q}, update controller λ online, and push into NestedReservoir with fixed keys and floors. Track Δ_spec, spectral tail, and validation residual norms.
5) For each budget, extract the top-K items from the reservoir (nested), form a weighted dataset, train the head (30 epochs), evaluate accuracy/NLL/ECE, and log time/memory.
6) Run baselines with identical training/eval; compute statistics.

Key expected outcomes
- At 1–10% budgets, OP-S&V improves test accuracy and reduces NLL and ECE compared to baselines; at 25–50%, OP-S&V remains competitive or better.
- Selection time near Random (1.2–1.5×) and 3–10× faster than Herding; bounded memory by O(r·d_hat) with r≤64.
- Ablations show r_i contributes most at small budgets; Fisher P reduces NLL/ECE meaningfully.

Minimal code sketch (selection core; linear head on precomputed features)

import torch, math, random, time
import numpy as np
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader

# Assume we have precomputed features: X_train: [N,D], y_train: [N], indices: [N]
# and X_val, y_val; X_test, y_test. Head: nn.Linear(D, C, bias=False).

class FeatureDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, X, y, idx):
        self.X = X; self.y = y; self.idx = idx
    def __len__(self): return self.X.size(0)
    def __getitem__(self, i): return self.X[i], self.y[i], self.idx[i]

def one_hot(y, C):
    return torch.nn.functional.one_hot(y, C).float()

def per_sample_head_grad(logits, y):
    # residuals: p - y
    p = torch.softmax(logits, dim=1)
    return p - one_hot(y, logits.size(1))

def countsketch(M, s_idx, s_sign, d_hat):
    B, D = M.shape
    out = M.new_zeros(B, d_hat)
    out.index_add_(1, s_idx, M * s_sign)
    return out

class RFD:
    def __init__(self, d_hat, r_max=64, forgetting=0.98, device='cuda'):
        self.d_hat = d_hat
        self.r_max = r_max
        self.forgetting = forgetting
        self.device = device
        self.B = torch.zeros(d_hat, r_max, device=device)
        self.rank = 0
        self.prev_proj = None
    @torch.no_grad()
    def update(self, X):  # X: [B, d_hat]
        self.B.mul_(math.sqrt(self.forgetting))
        Y = torch.cat([self.B, X.T], dim=1)  # [d_hat, r_max+B]
        # Economy QR then SVD on R for stability
        Q, R = torch.linalg.qr(Y, mode='reduced')
        U, S, Vh = torch.linalg.svd(R, full_matrices=False)
        # Shrinkage as in FD (robustness from forgetting + clipping)
        k = min(self.r_max, U.size(1))
        S2 = S[:k]**2
        tau = S2[k-1].item() if k>0 else 0.0
        S_shrink = torch.clamp(S2 - tau, min=0.0).sqrt()
        keep = (S_shrink > 1e-6)
        k_new = keep.sum().item()
        if k_new == 0:
            return 0.0, 0.0
        self.rank = k_new
        self.B[:, :k_new] = (Q @ U[:, :k_new]) * S_shrink[:k_new]
        # Δ_spec approx via projector difference
        U_basis, _ = torch.linalg.qr(self.B[:, :k_new], mode='reduced')
        proj = U_basis @ U_basis.T
        if self.prev_proj is None:
            delta = 0.0
        else:
            delta = torch.linalg.norm(proj - self.prev_proj, ord='fro').item()
        self.prev_proj = proj
        tail = (S_shrink[k_new:]**2).sum().item() if k_new < len(S_shrink) else 0.0
        return tail, delta
    def basis(self):
        if self.rank == 0:
            return torch.zeros(self.d_hat, 1, device=self.B.device)
        Q, _ = torch.linalg.qr(self.B[:, :self.rank], mode='reduced')
        return Q

class NestedReservoir:
    def __init__(self, max_k, seed=0):
        self.max_k = max_k
        self._heap = []  # (-priority, idx, weight)
        self.rng = random.Random(seed)
    def push(self, idx, weight, priority):
        import heapq
        item = (-priority, int(idx), float(weight))
        if len(self._heap) < self.max_k:
            heapq.heappush(self._heap, item)
        else:
            if item > self._heap[0]:
                heapq.heapreplace(self._heap, item)
    def topk(self, k):
        # return top-k items sorted by priority
        items = sorted(self._heap)
        return [(idx, w) for _, idx, w in items[:k]]

@torch.no_grad()
def op_snv_select(stream_loader, head, d_hat, fisher_diag, v_acc, v_cal, s_idx, s_sign,
                  eps_spec=0.05, eps_val=0.02, gamma=5e-4, budgets=(0.01,0.05,0.1,0.25,0.5)):
    device = next(head.parameters()).device
    rfd = RFD(d_hat=d_hat, r_max=64, forgetting=0.98, device=device)
    # Prepare projections of validation grads (preconditioned + CountSketch then project)
    P_inv = 1.0 / (fisher_diag + 1e-6)
    def sketch_and_project(v):
        vP = v * P_inv  # apply preconditioner
        v_cs = countsketch(vP[None, :], s_idx, s_sign, d_hat)[0]
        U = rfd.basis()
        return U @ (U.T @ v_cs)
    v_acc_proj = sketch_and_project(v_acc)
    v_cal_proj = sketch_and_project(v_cal)
    nA = v_acc_proj.norm() + 1e-8
    nC = v_cal_proj.norm() + 1e-8
    lam_acc, lam_cal = 1.0, 1.0
    ema_acc, ema_cal = 0.0, 0.0
    var_acc, var_cal = 0.0, 0.0
    beta = 0.9
    # Reservoir sized for max budget
    N_stream = len(stream_loader.dataset)
    max_k = int(max(budgets) * N_stream)
    reservoir = NestedReservoir(max_k=max_k, seed=13)
    start = time.time(); peak_mem = 0
    for Xb, yb, idxb in stream_loader:
        Xb = Xb.to(device); yb = yb.to(device)
        logits = Xb @ head.weight.T  # [B,C]
        res = per_sample_head_grad(logits, yb)  # [B,C]
        # Flatten per-sample grad wrt W: kron(res_i, X_i)
        g = torch.einsum('bc,bd->bcd', res, Xb).reshape(Xb.size(0), -1)
        g_cs = countsketch(g, s_idx, s_sign, d_hat)
        # Update RFD with mini-batch sketch for stability
        tail, delta = rfd.update(g_cs)
        U = rfd.basis()
        proj = g_cs @ U @ U.T
        novelty = (g_cs - proj).pow(2).sum(dim=1)
        acc_imp = torch.clamp(-(g_cs @ v_acc_proj) / nA, min=0)
        cal_imp = torch.clamp(-(g_cs @ v_cal_proj) / nC, min=0)
        score = novelty + lam_acc * acc_imp + lam_cal * cal_imp
        # Fixed-key priority sampling with floors
        for s, idxi in zip(score, idxb):
            key = (hash(int(idxi)) & 0xffffffff) / 2**32
            priority = (s.item() + 1e-8) / max(gamma, 1e-8) / max(key, 1e-9)
            reservoir.push(int(idxi), 1.0, priority)
        # Controller updates using batch projections (moments)
        bA = (g_cs @ v_acc_proj).mean().item()
        bC = (g_cs @ v_cal_proj).mean().item()
        prev_ema_acc = ema_acc; prev_ema_cal = ema_cal
        ema_acc = beta*ema_acc + (1-beta)*bA
        ema_cal = beta*ema_cal + (1-beta)*bC
        var_acc = beta*var_acc + (1-beta)*(bA - prev_ema_acc)**2
        var_cal = beta*var_cal + (1-beta)*(bC - prev_ema_cal)**2
        tau_acc, tau_cal = -0.01, -0.005
        step = 0.1
        lam_acc = float(np.clip(lam_acc + step * (ema_acc - tau_acc), 0.0, 10.0))
        lam_cal = float(np.clip(lam_cal + step * (ema_cal - tau_cal), 0.0, 10.0))
        # Stability guard on floors
        if var_acc > 2.0*np.median([1e-8, var_acc]) or var_cal > 2.0*np.median([1e-8, var_cal]):
            gamma = min(5e-3, gamma*2)
        # Track peak memory
        if torch.cuda.is_available():
            peak_mem = max(peak_mem, torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated())
    sel_time = time.time() - start
    return reservoir, sel_time, peak_mem

# Warm-up Fisher and validation gradients utilities

def compute_fisher_diag(loader, head, num_classes):
    device = next(head.parameters()).device
    D = head.weight.numel()
    fisher = torch.zeros(D, device=device)
    for Xb, yb, _ in loader:
        Xb, yb = Xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
        logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
        res = per_sample_head_grad(logits, yb)
        g = torch.einsum('bc,bd->bcd', res, Xb).reshape(Xb.size(0), -1)
        fisher += (g*g).mean(dim=0)
    fisher /= len(loader)
    return fisher

def val_grad(loader, head, objective='acc', temperature=1.0):
    device = next(head.parameters()).device
    head.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
    loss_total = 0.0
    for Xb, yb, _ in loader:
        Xb, yb = Xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
        logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
        if objective == 'acc' or objective == 'cal':
            # For cal, apply temperature scaling in the loss
            logits_scaled = logits / (temperature if objective=='cal' else 1.0)
            loss = torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy(logits_scaled, yb, reduction='sum')
        loss_total = loss_total + loss
    loss_total.backward()
    return head.weight.grad.reshape(-1).detach()


Evaluation/training snippet for head (shared across experiments)

def train_head(train_loader, val_loader, head, sample_weights=None, epochs=30, seed=13):
    torch.manual_seed(seed)
    device = next(head.parameters()).device
    opt = torch.optim.AdamW(head.parameters(), lr=2e-4, weight_decay=1e-2)
    sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=epochs)
    scaler = torch.cuda.amp.GradScaler() if torch.cuda.is_available() else None
    C = head.weight.size(0)
    head.train()
    for ep in range(epochs):
        for i,(Xb, yb, idxb) in enumerate(train_loader):
            Xb, yb = Xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
            w = None
            if sample_weights is not None:
                # assume sample_weights is dict idx->weight
                w = torch.tensor([sample_weights[int(i)] for i in idxb], device=device)
            opt.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
            with torch.cuda.amp.autocast(enabled=scaler is not None):
                logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
                loss = torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy(logits, yb, reduction='none')
                if w is not None:
                    loss = (loss * w)
                loss = loss.mean()
            if scaler is not None:
                scaler.scale(loss).backward()
                scaler.step(opt); scaler.update()
            else:
                loss.backward(); opt.step()
        sched.step()
    # Eval
    head.eval(); correct=0; n=0; nll=0.0
    with torch.no_grad():
        for Xb, yb, _ in val_loader:
            Xb, yb = Xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
            logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
            nll += torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy(logits, yb, reduction='sum').item()
            pred = logits.argmax(dim=1)
            correct += (pred==yb).sum().item(); n += yb.numel()
    return correct/n, nll/n


Experiment 2 — Multi-objective Fairness + Calibration on Text (constraint-first alignment)
Goal
- Demonstrate OP-S&V can align to fairness and calibration concurrently via a single validation pass and online controller, improving worst-group metrics and EOD gap without large overall accuracy loss at matched budgets.

Setup
- Dataset: CivilComments-WILDS (toxicity classification). Use official train/val/test splits. Use identity subgroups provided by WILDS (gender, religion, etc.).
- Model: frozen roberta-base encoder (transformers) with trainable linear head on [CLS] pooled features (dim 768). Precompute features for all splits to speed up selection and training.
- Objectives: accuracy (cross-entropy), fairness surrogate (equalized odds via soft-TPR gap), calibration (ECE surrogate via temperature-scaled NLL).

OP-S&V configuration (text)
- Warm-up: 0.5 epoch on 20% of training; head-only. Capture per-sample head grads snapshots and feed CountSketch(d_hat=2048)→RFD(r≤64, forgetting=0.98). Estimate Fisher diag.
- Validation: compute v_acc, v_cal, and v_group for each protected group g using soft-TPR gap on validation. Single backward per objective. Project with P and into U; concatenate objectives for controller.
- Probability floors: ensure per-group minimum selection probability (e.g., ≥1%) by raising scores or using per-group γ_g floors proportional to group prevalence.
- Controller: λ over objectives [acc, cal, groups 1..G]. Targets τ_q set to encourage negative projected residuals per objective; λ updated with projected subgradient. Stability guard identical to Experiment 1.

Baselines
- Random stratified by subgroup proportion.
- LossTop-k (global) and GroupLossTop-k (quota-based: allocate k proportionally to validation group importance or equal per group).
- EL2N/GraNd (computed once) with per-group rebalancing via quotas.

Metrics
- Overall ROC-AUC and accuracy; subgroup AUC; worst-group accuracy; equalized odds gap (average |TPR_g − TPR_all| and |FPR_g − FPR_all|); ECE (15 bins); selection time/memory.
- Pareto analysis: worst-group accuracy vs overall accuracy across budgets {2%, 5%, 10%, 25%}.
- Ablations: without group v_q; without probability floors; accuracy-only controller.

Procedure
1) Precompute RoBERTa features: tokenize with transformers; feed to roberta-base; save CLS-pooled vectors. Build PyTorch Datasets from features, labels, and group ids.
2) Warm-up on 20% subsample: train head for 0.5 epoch; capture per-sample grads and feed RFD; compute Fisher diag.
3) Validation gradients: compute v_acc and v_cal. For fairness, define soft-TPR per group and all, and L_fair = mean_g |TPR_g − TPR_all| + |FPR_g − FPR_all| using differentiable approximations (sigmoid on logits with α=5). Backprop once per fairness objective to get gradients v_group,g (or aggregate fairness as one vector and also per-group if needed). Project with P and into U.
4) Streaming pass: for each sample, compute g_{i,T}, novelty r_i, impacts a_{i,acc}, a_{i,cal}, and a_{i,g} for groups using projected validation gradients; apply per-group floors; update λ online; push into NestedReservoir.
5) For each budget, train head on weighted subset; evaluate overall and worst-group metrics, EOD gap, and ECE on test.

Key expected outcomes
- OP-S&V with fairness-aware controller improves worst-group accuracy and reduces EOD gap at small budgets (2–10%) with limited overall accuracy drop vs accuracy-only baselines; calibration (ECE) improves versus quotas-only baselines.

Minimal code sketch — computing group fairness gradients on validation

# logits: [N], y in {0,1}, groups: [N] in {0..G-1}
def soft_rate(logits, mask, positive=True, alpha=5.0):
    z = logits[mask]
    if z.numel() == 0:
        return logits.new_tensor(0.5)
    s = torch.sigmoid(alpha * z)
    if positive:
        return s.mean()
    else:
        return (1 - s).mean()

def fairness_loss_pairwise(logits, y, groups, G, alpha=5.0):
    # Approximate EOD via TPR/FPR gaps
    m_all_pos = (y == 1)
    m_all_neg = (y == 0)
    tpr_all = soft_rate(logits[m_all_pos], torch.ones_like(y[m_all_pos], dtype=torch.bool), positive=True, alpha=alpha)
    fpr_all = soft_rate(logits[m_all_neg], torch.ones_like(y[m_all_neg], dtype=torch.bool), positive=False, alpha=alpha)
    loss = logits.new_tensor(0.0)
    for g in range(G):
        mg = (groups == g)
        tpr_g = soft_rate(logits[mg & m_all_pos], torch.ones_like(y[mg & m_all_pos], dtype=torch.bool), positive=True, alpha=alpha)
        fpr_g = soft_rate(logits[mg & m_all_neg], torch.ones_like(y[mg & m_all_neg], dtype=torch.bool), positive=False, alpha=alpha)
        loss = loss + (tpr_g - tpr_all).abs() + (fpr_g - fpr_all).abs()
    return loss / G

# To obtain v_group: run a single backward on fairness_loss_pairwise over validation
# For multi-objective gradients: call backward separately for acc, cal, and fairness (clearing grad between calls), store head.weight.grad.view(-1).


Experiment 3 — Streaming, Drift, DP, and Mergeable Reservoirs (operational robustness)
Goal
- Validate continual refresh under distribution shift with drift-triggered validation re-alignment, mergeable reservoirs across shards, and differential privacy (DP) with bounded utility loss.

Setup
- Data stream: CIFAR-10 followed by CIFAR-10C (severity 3). Stream A: 40k clean (arrive in four 10k shards). Stream B: 10k per corruption type (Gaussian noise, blur), delivered in 5k shards (e.g., two corruptions → four shards). Use precomputed CLIP features for all shards.
- Model: frozen CLIP ViT-B/32 + linear head.
- Privacy: Gaussian noise to sketch updates and to validation gradients; target (ε, δ) ≈ (2.0, 1e−6). Use opacus RDP accountant to calibrate σ.

OP-S&V configuration (streaming/DP)
- RFD: forgetting=0.97. Monitor Δ_spec; trigger validation re-computation if Δ_spec>κ=0.2 or every 5k samples (whichever first). Maintain controller λ across shards; widen γ during high-variance phases.
- NestedReservoir per shard: build local reservoirs with fixed keys; merge via priority-union into a global reservoir preserving nestedness/unbiasedness.
- DP: sketch updates X_t and validation gradients v_q are perturbed with Gaussian noise N(0, σ² I). Clip sketch rows to L2 norm C before noise. Use opacus to compute ε for given σ, T updates, and delta; choose σ ensuring ε≤2.0.

Baselines
- Streaming EL2N recomputed at each shard (resets early-loss; no nestedness guarantee).
- Random streaming with equal per-shard budgets.

Metrics
- Accuracy on clean and corrupted test sets after training on the current global reservoir at milestones (after each shard). Also track NLL.
- Projected validation residual norm before/after refresh as a proxy for cumulative validation regret.
- Δ_spec traces versus performance; number/timing of refreshes; selection time/memory.
- DP utility: compare DP vs non-DP OP-S&V at budgets {5%, 10%, 25%}.

Procedure
1) Process Stream A shards to build reservoir_A; after last shard, finetune head and evaluate on clean and corruption tests.
2) As Stream B shards arrive, continue RFD with forgetting; compute Δ_spec each mini-batch. When Δ_spec>κ or period elapses, recompute v_q on a small mixed validation set (e.g., 2.5k clean + 2.5k corrupted) and reset controller moments (not λ). Resume streaming.
3) For each shard, build a local reservoir_s; after shard completion, merge with global reservoir via priority-union; checkpoint and evaluate.
4) Repeat entire pipeline with DP noise enabled; adjust σ using opacus RDP accountant for T updates to meet ε≈2.0, δ=1e−6.

Key expected outcomes
- Δ_spec spikes when corruption stream begins; re-validation reduces projected residual and improves corrupted-test accuracy versus not refreshing.
- Merged reservoirs perform within CI of a single monolithic run.
- DP adds small accuracy drop (<2–3 pp at 10% budget) while maintaining nestedness and operational stability.

Minimal code sketch — drift trigger, DP noise, and reservoir merge

import heapq
from opacus.accountants.rdp import RDPAccountant

class DPSketch:
    def __init__(self, C=1.0, sigma=0.5):
        self.C = C; self.sigma = sigma
    def privatize(self, X):
        # clip and add Gaussian noise
        norms = X.norm(dim=1, keepdim=True).clamp_min(1e-6)
        Xc = X * (self.C / norms).clamp(max=1.0)
        noise = torch.randn_like(Xc) * self.sigma
        return Xc + noise

def priority_union(resA, resB, K):
    merged = NestedReservoir(max_k=K)
    heap = resA._heap + resB._heap
    heapq.heapify(heap)
    while len(merged._heap) < K and heap:
        merged._heap.append(heapq.heappop(heap))
    return merged

# RDP-based sigma calibration

def calibrate_sigma(epsilon_target=2.0, delta=1e-6, T=1000, q=1.0):
    # q is sampling rate per update; for full-batch sketch updates over B rows, set q=1.0.
    # Simple grid search over sigma
    for sigma in np.linspace(0.1, 2.0, 20):
        acc = RDPAccountant()
        for t in range(T):
            acc.step(mechanism='Gaussian', noise_multiplier=sigma, sample_rate=q)
        eps = acc.get_epsilon(delta=delta)
        if eps <= epsilon_target:
            return sigma
    return 2.0

# Shard processing with drift trigger

def process_shard(shard_loader, head, rfd, dps: DPSketch, controller_state, reservoir_global, val_mix_loader, s_idx, s_sign, d_hat, gamma=5e-4):
    device = next(head.parameters()).device
    local_res = NestedReservoir(max_k=int(0.25*len(shard_loader.dataset)))  # capacity placeholder
    for Xb, yb, idxb in shard_loader:
        Xb, yb = Xb.to(device), yb.to(device)
        logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
        res = per_sample_head_grad(logits, yb)
        g = torch.einsum('bc,bd->bcd', res, Xb).reshape(Xb.size(0), -1)
        g_cs = countsketch(g, s_idx, s_sign, d_hat)
        if dps is not None:
            g_cs = dps.privatize(g_cs)
        tail, delta = rfd.update(g_cs)
        if delta > 0.2:
            # recompute validation gradients on mixed val; reset controller moments
            controller_state.refresh(val_mix_loader)
        # compute scores via controller_state.score(g_cs) and push to local_res
        # ... omitted for brevity
    # merge
    K = reservoir_global.max_k
    merged = priority_union(reservoir_global, local_res, K)
    return merged


Integration details used across experiments (to enhance reliability and avoid redundancy)
- Data pipelines
  • Images: CIFAR-100/CIFAR-10/CIFAR-10C loaded via torchvision; resize to 224×224 for CLIP; normalize using CLIP mean/std. For CIFAR-10C, use official corrupted images or generate via robustness libraries to avoid inconsistencies across seeds.
  • Text: CivilComments-WILDS loaded via wilds; tokenize with transformers AutoTokenizer; truncate/pad to max_len=256.
- Feature precomputation
  • open_clip: create model, preprocess, and extract pooled features with no grad; save torch.float16 tensors to reduce disk.
  • transformers (RoBERTa): forward in eval mode with no grad; CLS-pooled last hidden state; save features.
- Hashes for CountSketch and fixed keys: create s_idx and s_sign from a seeded numpy RandomState; store with artifacts for reproducibility.
- Controller state and scores
  • Maintain running estimates of E_p[⟨g, Pv̂_q⟩] with EMA; track variance with Welford or EMA-of-squared-diffs; adapt γ as described.
  • Score per sample: s_i = r_i + Σ_q λ_q a_{i,q}, with a_{i,q}=max(−⟨g_cs, v̂_q⟩,0)/||v̂_q||.
  • Weights for training: w_i = 1/(m p_i) with 95th percentile clipping.
- Auto-stop
  • Stop selection early if spectral tail<eps_spec and projected validation residual norms drop below eps_val for all objectives; useful in streams with abundant redundancy.
- Baseline implementations
  • GraNd: compute per-sample grad norm of head at warm-up end; use torch.autograd.grad with create_graph=False across batches for stability, but for head-only, closed-form residual×feature is faster.
  • EL2N: average CE over warm-up steps; cache per-sample running mean.
  • Herding (k-center greedy): use faiss GPU IndexFlatIP on normalized features or sklearn pairwise distances; greedy farthest-point selection to k points.
- Calibration/ECE
  • Use netcal or a small custom function; for T calibration, one-dimensional LBFGS on validation NLL is sufficient.
- Statistical testing
  • For each budget, across seeds, collect paired metric arrays for OP-S&V and the strongest baseline; use ttest_rel; report p-values and effect sizes (Cohen’s d).

Ablations and diagnostics (distributed across experiments to avoid redundancy)
- In Experiment 1, ablate r_i and P; in Experiment 2, ablate group gradients and floors; in Experiment 3, ablate drift-trigger refresh and DP noise levels.
- Diagnostics captured everywhere: spectral tail, Δ_spec, λ trajectories, projected residual norms, coverage by group (Exp2), nestedness violations (all), and selection memory/time.

Risk mitigations
- If RFD becomes numerically unstable, fall back to sklearn IncrementalPCA with a sliding window and forgetting factor; log the fallback and continue.
- If per-sample gradients slow throughput, restrict to head-only (as specified), precompute features, and use einsum-based gradients; if necessary, switch to functorch.grad_and_value with vmap for more complex heads.
- If fairness gradients are noisy (small groups), enlarge validation or apply EMA smoothing on v_group vectors across refreshes.

What to submit/log per experiment for reproducibility
- Config (JSON): seeds, budgets, d_hat, r_max, forgetting, eps_spec, eps_val, τ targets, γ schedule.
- Hashes and keys: s_idx/s_sign seeds, reservoir key seed.
- Artifacts: U basis tensors, Fisher diag, projected v̂_q tensors, λ logs, reservoir indices/weights per budget.
- Raw metrics and plots: accuracy/NLL/ECE vs budget (Exp1), Pareto curves and worst-group metrics (Exp2), Δ_spec and residual norms with shard boundaries and refresh events (Exp3), DP epsilon achieved and utility gap.

Expected consolidated impact across experiments
- Demonstrate a practical, single-pass, validation-aligned selector that produces reusable, nested subsets across budgets with strong small-budget accuracy and calibration (Exp1), fairness-aware control (Exp2), and operational robustness with streaming, drift, and DP (Exp3).
experimental_design.experiment_code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
"""
One-Pass Sketch-and-Validate (OP-S&V) — Experiments 1–3

This script implements the OP-S&V method (head-only) together with baselines and evaluation/plotting utilities,
following the provided New Methods, Experiment Strategy, and Experiment Specification.

It includes:
- Core modules: CountSketch, Robust Frequent Directions (RFD) with forgetting, validation-aligned gradient projection,
  online dual controller (simplified), nested reservoir with fixed keys and priority-merge (for streaming), and DP noise wrapper.
- Experiments 1–3 with synthetic fallbacks for quick functionality tests.
- Baselines: Random, LossTop-k, EntropyTop-k, EL2N, GraNd, and optional Herding/k-center greedy (if sklearn is available).
- Plotting (matplotlib/seaborn): accuracy vs budgets, ECE vs budgets, training loss curves, confusion matrix, Pareto front (fairness),
  drift index and streaming accuracy curves. All plots are saved as .pdf using plt.savefig(..., bbox_inches="tight").

Required Python libraries that are likely needed to run the full experiments (install as needed):
- torch (>=2.0)
- torchvision (for real image datasets; optional in this script)
- numpy
- scipy (for stats like t-tests; optional in this script)
- matplotlib
- seaborn
- scikit-learn (optional; for Herding/k-center greedy)
- transformers (optional; for real text models/features)
- wilds (optional; for CivilComments-WILDS dataset)
- faiss-gpu or faiss-cpu (optional; for fast k-center; sklearn fallback is provided)
- opacus (optional; for DP RDP accountant; fallback provided)
- open_clip_torch (optional; for CLIP features)

Note: The provided quick test uses only torch, numpy, matplotlib, and seaborn with synthetic data so it completes quickly.

Usage:
- Run this file directly to execute quick tests: python op_snv_experiments.py
- Integrate real datasets by replacing synthetic data generation with real feature extraction/loading.
"""

import os
import time
import math
import json
import random
from typing import Dict, List, Tuple, Optional

import numpy as np
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader

import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

try:
    from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix
    HAVE_SKLEARN = True
except Exception:
    HAVE_SKLEARN = False

# ------------------------------
# Reproducibility and device
# ------------------------------

def set_seed(seed: int = 13):
    random.seed(seed)
    np.random.seed(seed)
    torch.manual_seed(seed)
    if torch.cuda.is_available():
        torch.cuda.manual_seed_all(seed)


def get_device():
    return torch.device("cuda") if torch.cuda.is_available() else torch.device("cpu")


# ------------------------------
# Datasets (feature-level)
# ------------------------------

class FeatureDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, X: torch.Tensor, y: torch.Tensor, idx: torch.Tensor,
                 groups: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None):
        self.X = X
        self.y = y
        self.idx = idx
        self.groups = groups
    def __len__(self):
        return self.X.size(0)
    def __getitem__(self, i):
        if self.groups is None:
            return self.X[i], self.y[i], self.idx[i]
        else:
            return self.X[i], self.y[i], self.idx[i], self.groups[i]


# ------------------------------
# CountSketch and hashing utils
# ------------------------------

def create_s_idx_sign(P: int, d_hat: int, seed: int = 13, device=None):
    rng = np.random.RandomState(seed)
    s_idx = torch.tensor(rng.randint(0, d_hat, size=(P,), dtype=np.int64), dtype=torch.long, device=device)
    s_sign = torch.tensor(rng.choice([-1.0, 1.0], size=(P,), replace=True), dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
    return s_idx, s_sign


def countsketch(M: torch.Tensor, s_idx: torch.Tensor, s_sign: torch.Tensor, d_hat: int) -> torch.Tensor:
    # M: [B, P]
    B, P = M.shape
    out = M.new_zeros(B, d_hat)
    out.index_add_(1, s_idx, M * s_sign)
    return out


# ------------------------------
# Robust Frequent Directions (RFD) with forgetting (simplified)
# ------------------------------

class RFD:
    def __init__(self, d_hat: int, r_max: int = 64, forgetting: float = 0.98, device=None):
        self.d_hat = d_hat
        self.r_max = r_max
        self.forgetting = forgetting
        self.device = device or get_device()
        self.B = torch.zeros(d_hat, r_max, device=self.device)
        self.rank = 0
        self.prev_proj = None
    @torch.no_grad()
    def update(self, X: torch.Tensor) -> Tuple[float, float]:
        # X: [B, d_hat]
        self.B.mul_(math.sqrt(self.forgetting))
        Y = torch.cat([self.B, X.T], dim=1)  # [d_hat, r_max + B]
        Q, R = torch.linalg.qr(Y, mode='reduced')
        U, S, Vh = torch.linalg.svd(R, full_matrices=False)
        k = min(self.r_max, U.size(1))
        S2 = S[:k]**2
        tau = S2[k-1].item() if k > 0 else 0.0
        S_shrink = torch.clamp(S2 - tau, min=0.0).sqrt()
        keep = (S_shrink > 1e-6)
        k_new = int(keep.sum().item())
        if k_new == 0:
            return 0.0, 0.0
        self.rank = k_new
        self.B[:, :k_new] = (Q @ U[:, :k_new]) * S_shrink[:k_new]
        # Δ_spec approximation via projector difference
        U_basis, _ = torch.linalg.qr(self.B[:, :k_new], mode='reduced')
        proj = U_basis @ U_basis.T
        if self.prev_proj is None:
            delta = 0.0
        else:
            delta = torch.linalg.norm(proj - self.prev_proj, ord='fro').item()
        self.prev_proj = proj
        tail = (S_shrink[k_new:]**2).sum().item() if k_new < len(S_shrink) else 0.0
        return tail, delta
    def basis(self) -> torch.Tensor:
        if self.rank == 0:
            return torch.zeros(self.d_hat, 1, device=self.B.device)
        Q, _ = torch.linalg.qr(self.B[:, :self.rank], mode='reduced')
        return Q


# ------------------------------
# Nested reservoir sampling with fixed keys
# ------------------------------

class NestedReservoir:
    def __init__(self, max_k: int, seed: int = 13):
        self.max_k = max_k
        self._heap = []  # stores (-priority, idx, weight)
        self.seed = seed
        self.rng = random.Random(seed)
    def push(self, idx: int, weight: float, priority: float):
        import heapq
        item = (-float(priority), int(idx), float(weight))
        if len(self._heap) < self.max_k:
            heapq.heappush(self._heap, item)
        else:
            if item > self._heap[0]:
                heapq.heapreplace(self._heap, item)
    def topk(self, k: int) -> List[Tuple[int, float]]:
        items = sorted(self._heap)  # ascending by -priority -> descending by priority
        return [(idx, w) for (_, idx, w) in items[:k]]
    def size(self) -> int:
        return len(self._heap)
    def merge(self, other: 'NestedReservoir') -> 'NestedReservoir':
        # priority-union merge (keeps highest-priority items)
        import heapq
        merged = NestedReservoir(max_k=self.max_k, seed=self.seed)
        heap = self._heap + other._heap
        heapq.heapify(heap)
        while len(merged._heap) < merged.max_k and len(heap) > 0:
            merged._heap.append(heapq.heappop(heap))
        return merged


def verify_nestedness(budget_indices: Dict[float, List[int]]) -> float:
    # returns fraction of (B > b) pairs that are nested (1.0 means perfect nestedness)
    budgets = sorted(budget_indices.keys())
    total_checks, ok = 0, 0
    prev_set = None
    for b in budgets:
        cur = set(budget_indices[b])
        if prev_set is not None:
            total_checks += 1
            if prev_set.issubset(cur):
                ok += 1
        prev_set = cur
    return (ok / total_checks) if total_checks > 0 else 1.0


# ------------------------------
# Gradients, Fisher, validation gradients
# ------------------------------

def per_sample_head_residual(logits: torch.Tensor, y: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Returns residuals (p - y_onehot) of shape [B, C]
    p = torch.softmax(logits, dim=1)
    y_onehot = F.one_hot(y, logits.size(1)).float()
    return p - y_onehot


def flatten_head_grads(residuals: torch.Tensor, features: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    # residuals [B,C], features [B,D] -> [B, C*D]
    return torch.einsum('bc,bd->bcd', residuals, features).reshape(features.size(0), -1)


def compute_fisher_diag(loader: DataLoader, head: nn.Linear) -> torch.Tensor:
    device = next(head.parameters()).device
    P = head.weight.numel()
    fisher = torch.zeros(P, device=device)
    count = 0
    for batch in loader:
        if len(batch) == 3:
            Xb, yb, _ = batch
        else:
            Xb, yb, _ = batch[0], batch[1], batch[2]
        Xb = Xb.to(device); yb = yb.to(device)
        logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
        res = per_sample_head_residual(logits, yb)
        g = flatten_head_grads(res, Xb)
        fisher += (g * g).mean(dim=0)
        count += 1
    if count > 0:
        fisher /= float(count)
    return fisher


@torch.no_grad()
def validation_gradient(loader: DataLoader, head: nn.Linear, objective: str = 'acc', temperature: float = 1.0) -> torch.Tensor:
    device = next(head.parameters()).device
    head.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
    loss_total = 0.0
    for batch in loader:
        if len(batch) == 3:
            Xb, yb, _ = batch
        else:
            Xb, yb, _ = batch[0], batch[1], batch[2]
        Xb = Xb.to(device); yb = yb.to(device)
        logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
        if objective in ['acc', 'cal']:
            logits_scaled = logits / (temperature if objective == 'cal' else 1.0)
            loss = F.cross_entropy(logits_scaled, yb, reduction='sum')
        else:
            raise ValueError("Unknown objective for validation_gradient: %s" % objective)
        loss_total = loss_total + loss
    # Backprop once
    loss_total.backward()
    v = head.weight.grad.view(-1).detach()
    head.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
    return v


# ------------------------------
# Calibration and ECE
# ------------------------------

def temperature_scale_grid(logits: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, T_grid: np.ndarray) -> float:
    best_T, best_nll = 1.0, float('inf')
    for T in T_grid:
        p = torch.softmax(torch.tensor(logits / T), dim=1).numpy()
        # Negative log likelihood
        nll = -np.log(p[np.arange(len(y)), y] + 1e-12).mean()
        if nll < best_nll:
            best_nll = nll
            best_T = float(T)
    return best_T


def compute_ece(probs: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray, n_bins: int = 15) -> float:
    # probs: [N, C], y: [N]
    confidences = probs.max(axis=1)
    predictions = probs.argmax(axis=1)
    accuracies = (predictions == y).astype(float)
    bins = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, n_bins + 1)
    ece = 0.0
    for i in range(n_bins):
        m = (confidences > bins[i]) & (confidences <= bins[i+1])
        if m.sum() > 0:
            acc = accuracies[m].mean()
            conf = confidences[m].mean()
            ece += (m.mean()) * abs(acc - conf)
    return float(ece)


# ------------------------------
# OP-S&V selection (head-only)
# ------------------------------

def op_snv_select(
    stream_loader: DataLoader,
    head: nn.Linear,
    d_hat: int,
    fisher_diag: torch.Tensor,
    v_acc: torch.Tensor,
    v_cal: torch.Tensor,
    s_idx: torch.Tensor,
    s_sign: torch.Tensor,
    budgets: Tuple[float, ...] = (0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5),
    gamma: float = 5e-4,
    forgetting: float = 0.98,
    r_max: int = 64,
    tau_acc: float = -0.01,
    tau_cal: float = -0.005,
    seed: int = 13,
) -> Tuple[NestedReservoir, float, int, Dict[str, List[float]]]:
    device = next(head.parameters()).device
    rfd = RFD(d_hat=d_hat, r_max=r_max, forgetting=forgetting, device=device)
    P_inv = 1.0 / (fisher_diag + 1e-6)

    def proj_vec(v: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        vP = v * P_inv
        v_cs = countsketch(vP[None, :], s_idx, s_sign, d_hat)[0]
        U = rfd.basis()
        return U @ (U.T @ v_cs)

    v_acc_proj = proj_vec(v_acc)
    v_cal_proj = proj_vec(v_cal)
    nA = v_acc_proj.norm() + 1e-8
    nC = v_cal_proj.norm() + 1e-8

    lam_acc, lam_cal = 1.0, 1.0
    ema_acc, ema_cal = 0.0, 0.0
    var_acc, var_cal = 0.0, 0.0
    beta = 0.9

    N_stream = len(stream_loader.dataset)
    max_k = int(max(budgets) * max(1, N_stream))
    reservoir = NestedReservoir(max_k=max_k, seed=seed)

    start = time.time()
    peak_mem = 0
    diag = {"lambda_acc": [], "lambda_cal": [], "delta_spec": [], "spectral_tail": []}

    for batch in stream_loader:
        Xb, yb, idxb = batch[0].to(device), batch[1].to(device), batch[2]
        logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
        res = per_sample_head_residual(logits, yb)
        g_flat = flatten_head_grads(res, Xb)
        g_cs = countsketch(g_flat, s_idx, s_sign, d_hat)
        tail, delta = rfd.update(g_cs)
        U = rfd.basis()
        proj = g_cs @ U @ U.T
        novelty = (g_cs - proj).pow(2).sum(dim=1)
        acc_imp = torch.clamp(-(g_cs @ v_acc_proj) / nA, min=0)
        cal_imp = torch.clamp(-(g_cs @ v_cal_proj) / nC, min=0)
        score = novelty + lam_acc * acc_imp + lam_cal * cal_imp
        for s, idxi in zip(score, idxb):
            key = (hash(int(idxi)) & 0xffffffff) / 2**32
            priority = float((s.item() + 1e-8) / max(gamma, 1e-8) / max(key, 1e-9))
            reservoir.push(int(idxi), 1.0, priority)
        # Controller updates
        bA = (g_cs @ v_acc_proj).mean().item()
        bC = (g_cs @ v_cal_proj).mean().item()
        prev_ema_acc, prev_ema_cal = ema_acc, ema_cal
        ema_acc = beta * ema_acc + (1 - beta) * bA
        ema_cal = beta * ema_cal + (1 - beta) * bC
        var_acc = beta * var_acc + (1 - beta) * (bA - prev_ema_acc) ** 2
        var_cal = beta * var_cal + (1 - beta) * (bC - prev_ema_cal) ** 2
        step = 0.1
        lam_acc = float(np.clip(lam_acc + step * (ema_acc - tau_acc), 0.0, 10.0))
        lam_cal = float(np.clip(lam_cal + step * (ema_cal - tau_cal), 0.0, 10.0))
        # Stability guard (increase floor when variance spikes)
        if var_acc > 10 * 1e-8 or var_cal > 10 * 1e-8:
            gamma = min(5e-3, gamma * 1.1)
        # Diagnostics
        diag["lambda_acc"].append(lam_acc)
        diag["lambda_cal"].append(lam_cal)
        diag["delta_spec"].append(delta)
        diag["spectral_tail"].append(tail)
        if torch.cuda.is_available():
            peak_mem = max(peak_mem, torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated())

    sel_time = time.time() - start
    return reservoir, sel_time, peak_mem, diag


# ------------------------------
# Baseline selectors
# ------------------------------

def select_random(N: int, budgets: Tuple[float, ...], seed: int = 13) -> Dict[float, List[int]]:
    rng = np.random.RandomState(seed)
    perm = rng.permutation(N)
    out = {}
    for b in budgets:
        k = int(max(1, b * N))
        out[b] = perm[:k].tolist()
    return out


def compute_ce_scores(X: torch.Tensor, y: torch.Tensor, head: nn.Linear) -> np.ndarray:
    with torch.no_grad():
        logits = X @ head.weight.T
        ce = F.cross_entropy(logits, y, reduction='none').cpu().numpy()
    return ce


def select_topk_by_scores(scores: np.ndarray, budgets: Tuple[float, ...]) -> Dict[float, List[int]]:
    order = np.argsort(-scores)  # descending
    N = len(scores)
    out = {}
    for b in budgets:
        k = int(max(1, b * N))
        out[b] = order[:k].tolist()
    return out


def compute_entropy_scores(X: torch.Tensor, head: nn.Linear) -> np.ndarray:
    with torch.no_grad():
        logits = X @ head.weight.T
        p = torch.softmax(logits, dim=1).cpu().numpy()
    ent = -(p * (np.log(p + 1e-12))).sum(axis=1)
    return ent


def compute_el2n_scores(X_list: List[torch.Tensor], y_list: List[torch.Tensor], head_list: List[nn.Linear]) -> np.ndarray:
    # Approximate EL2N: mean cross-entropy over provided checkpoints
    N = X_list[0].size(0)
    vals = np.zeros(N, dtype=np.float32)
    count = 0
    for X, y, head in zip(X_list, y_list, head_list):
        with torch.no_grad():
            logits = X @ head.weight.T
            ce = F.cross_entropy(logits, y, reduction='none').cpu().numpy()
        vals += ce
        count += 1
    vals /= max(1, count)
    return vals


def compute_grand_scores(X: torch.Tensor, y: torch.Tensor, head: nn.Linear) -> np.ndarray:
    with torch.no_grad():
        logits = X @ head.weight.T
        res = per_sample_head_residual(logits, y)
        g = flatten_head_grads(res, X)
        gn = g.norm(dim=1).cpu().numpy()
    return gn


def herding_kcenter_indices(X: np.ndarray, k: int, seed: int = 13) -> List[int]:
    # Simple farthest-first traversal (cosine distance on normalized features)
    N, D = X.shape
    Xn = X / (np.linalg.norm(X, axis=1, keepdims=True) + 1e-12)
    rng = np.random.RandomState(seed)
    start = rng.randint(0, N)
    centers = [start]
    dist = 1.0 - (Xn @ Xn[start])  # cosine distance
    for _ in range(1, k):
        idx = int(np.argmax(dist))
        centers.append(idx)
        dist = np.minimum(dist, 1.0 - (Xn @ Xn[idx]))
    return centers


# ------------------------------
# Training and evaluation (head-only)
# ------------------------------

def train_head(
    train_loader: DataLoader,
    val_loader: DataLoader,
    head: nn.Linear,
    sample_weights: Optional[Dict[int, float]] = None,
    epochs: int = 10,
    lr: float = 2e-3,
    wd: float = 1e-2,
    seed: int = 13,
    verbose: bool = False,
) -> Tuple[List[float], float, float]:
    set_seed(seed)
    device = next(head.parameters()).device
    opt = torch.optim.AdamW(head.parameters(), lr=lr, weight_decay=wd)
    sched = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=max(1, epochs))
    losses = []
    for ep in range(epochs):
        head.train()
        for batch in train_loader:
            Xb, yb, idxb = batch[0].to(device), batch[1].to(device), batch[2]
            opt.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
            logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
            loss = F.cross_entropy(logits, yb, reduction='none')
            if sample_weights is not None:
                w = torch.tensor([sample_weights.get(int(i), 1.0) for i in idxb], device=device)
                loss = (loss * w)
            loss = loss.mean()
            loss.backward()
            opt.step()
            losses.append(float(loss.item()))
        sched.step()
        if verbose:
            print(f"    [train_head] Epoch {ep+1}/{epochs} loss={losses[-1]:.4f}")
    # Eval
    head.eval()
    correct, n, nll = 0, 0, 0.0
    logits_all, y_all = [], []
    with torch.no_grad():
        for batch in val_loader:
            Xb, yb = batch[0].to(device), batch[1].to(device)
            logits = Xb @ head.weight.T
            nll += F.cross_entropy(logits, yb, reduction='sum').item()
            pred = logits.argmax(dim=1)
            correct += (pred == yb).sum().item()
            n += yb.numel()
            logits_all.append(logits.cpu())
            y_all.append(yb.cpu())
    acc = correct / max(1, n)
    nll = nll / max(1, n)
    logits_all = torch.cat(logits_all, dim=0).numpy()
    y_all = torch.cat(y_all, dim=0).numpy()
    return losses, acc, nll


def build_loader_from_indices(dataset: FeatureDataset, indices: List[int], batch_size: int = 256, shuffle: bool = True) -> DataLoader:
    sub = FeatureDataset(dataset.X[indices], dataset.y[indices], dataset.idx[indices], getattr(dataset, 'groups', None)[indices] if dataset.groups is not None else None)
    return DataLoader(sub, batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=shuffle, num_workers=0, pin_memory=False)


# ------------------------------
# Experiment 1: Images (features), accuracy/calibration/efficiency
